comparisons.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions w
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the source document: the two individuals named, their former employers, the specific violations (CARES Act loan fraud and embezzlement), and the enforcement action type (consent prohibition orders).

comparisons.title: Minutes of the Board's discount rate meeting on Ap
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the source document: meeting dates (April 20 and 29, 2026), discount rate review purpose, distinction from FOMC decisions, and transparency function are all explicitly stated.

comparisons.title: Kevin Warsh takes oath of office as chairman and a
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the source document: oath date (May 22, 2026), FOMC unanimous selection, Trump nomination (March 4), Senate confirmation dates (May 12-13), and term end dates (May 21, 2030 and January 31, 2040).

comparisons.title: Agencies publish resolution plan feedback letters
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the document: the agencies published feedback letters, reviewed 8 domestic and 56 foreign banks, found no deficiencies, and confirmed derivatives weaknesses were addressed.

comparisons.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action wi
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the source document: the Fed issued a consent prohibition order against Nakia R. Logan, a former Commerce Bank employee, for fraudulent customer transactions.

comparisons.title: Federal Reserve Board requests public comment on a
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims in the summary are directly supported by the document text: the payment account proposal, eligibility restrictions (no intraday credit, no discount window), and the lack of impact on existing Fed account holders.

comparisons.title: Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, Apri
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims in the summary are directly supported by the source document: meeting dates, publication timing, transparency purpose, and lack of regulatory changes.

comparisons.title: Federal Reserve Board names Jerome H. Powell as ch
comparisons.faithfulness_reason: All claims are directly supported by the source document: Powell's appointment as chair pro tempore, the temporary nature of the role, the transition to Warsh, and the consistency with past practice.
